description_long: This object consists of two black-bladed axes crossed behind a round wooden shield. The shield features a dark, natural wood grain texture with a black metal rim, giving it a robust and medieval appearance. The axe handles are made of brown wood, slightly curved, and fitted with black, double-edged blades that have a pronounced bearded shape. The front of the shield is plain, while the back reveals the handles of the axes and two vertical wooden grips attached to the shield. The overall design suggests a decorative or display piece, possibly inspired by Viking or fantasy motifs. The materials and construction evoke durability and a sense of historical craftsmanship.
description: A pair of black-bladed axes crossed behind a round, wood-grain shield with a black metal rim, featuring visible handles and grips on the back.
description_view_0: A narrow side view showing the thin profile of the shield and the axes, with minimal detail visible.
description_view_1: The front view displays the round wooden shield with a black rim and the two black axe blades extending outward symmetrically.
description_view_2: Another narrow side view, similar to view 0, showing the profile of the shield and axes.
description_view_3: The back view reveals the crossed wooden axe handles, the attachment points, and two vertical wooden grips on the shield.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handles with a rounded cross-section, smooth finish, and no visible grip texture.
attribute_blade_design: Double-edged, bearded black blades with a pronounced curve and pointed tips, no visible decorative engravings.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the wooden handle.
